tableRow.maxContractTerm: No statutory maximum found for regular public-school-district pupil-transportation contracts. Title 14 Ch. 29 sets no numeric cap; the operative unit is the school year, with contracts rolling forward via the annual letter-of-commitment mechanism (14 Del. C. § 2908(d)) and the § 2908(a) first-offer-of-renewal right. Practical horizon set by bus capital-allowance/age schedule (7-year new-bus allowance; 14-model-year replacement under § 2907 and Reg. 1150-21.0). For charter schools only, 14 Del. C. § 508(b) authorizes negotiated contracts for '1 or more years' with no upper bound stated.
tableRow.rebidRequired: Conditional. 14 Del. C. § 2908(a) requires renewal to be first offered to the incumbent contractor at expiration of any contract not terminated by bus-age limits. Competitive notice (published in at least 2 Delaware newspapers of general circulation) mandatory only when incumbent declines, bus aged out, or contractor fails to sign letter of commitment. § 2908(b) backstop: agency furnishes transportation directly if no replacement found. 29 Del. C. § 6904(c) exempts school-transportation contracts from Title 29 Ch. 69.
tableRow.renewalMechanics: Renewal without a full competitive rebid is the statutory default: (1) right-of-first-refusal renewal to incumbent (§ 2908(a)); (2) annual May 1 letter of commitment (§ 2908(d)(1)), contingent on succeeding fiscal year budget appropriation act; (3) letter of commitment is statutorily 'considered an offer of renewal' (§ 2908(d)(3)); (4) contract transfer permitted with local board and DOE approval (§ 2908(c)); (5) new contractor must offer to purchase existing bus at FMV if bus is under 7 years old and still on depreciation schedule (§ 2908(c)(4)); (6) no statutory CPI/index escalator for district contracts; (7) for charter schools only, § 508(b) permits fuel-adjustment provisions and multi-year contracts.
tableRow.districtVariance: Unknown. The § 2908 renewal-and-letter-of-commitment mechanism is administered centrally by DOE; no AG opinion, AOA report, DOE bulletin, or DSBA guidance documenting either uniform statewide application or material inter-district divergence located.
